skill.name: azure-security-keyvault-secrets-java
skill.description: Azure Key Vault Secrets Java SDK for secret management. Use when storing, retrieving, or managing passwords, API keys, connection strings, or other sensitive configuration data.
skill.summary: Manage secrets securely in Azure Key Vault using Java SDK with sync and async clients.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides documentation for the official Azure Key Vault Secrets Java SDK. All static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives. The detected patterns are XML declarations and Java code examples, not executable shell commands or malicious code. The skill is safe for publication.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Key Vault secrets with Java
content.value_statement: Securely store and retrieve sensitive data like passwords and API keys using Azure Key Vault. This skill provides Java SDK patterns for secret management with proper authentication and rotation practices.
actual_capabilities: Create synchronous and asynchronous Key Vault secret clients
Store, retrieve, update, and delete secrets in Azure Key Vault
List secrets and their versions with pagination support
Backup and restore secrets for disaster recovery
Configure secret properties like expiration dates and tags
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Key Vault resource
Cannot modify secret values - must create new versions
Requires proper Azure AD permissions for vault access
Network connectivity required to Azure Key Vault endpoint

use_cases.title: Enterprise Application Secrets
use_cases.description: Store database connection strings, API keys, and service credentials securely instead of in configuration files.
use_cases.target_user: Backend Java developers building cloud-native applications

use_cases.title: Secret Rotation Automation
use_cases.description: Implement automated credential rotation by creating new secret versions and deactivating old ones.

use_cases.title: Multi-Environment Configuration
use_cases.description: Load environment-specific secrets using tags and properties to support dev, staging, and production deployments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Secret Storage
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an Azure Key Vault secret client and store a database password using the Java SDK.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic secret operations

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve and Use Secret
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I retrieve a secret from Azure Key Vault and use its value in my Java application? Include error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Secret Rotation Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Java method that rotates a secret by disabling the old version and creating a new one with an updated value.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Secret Loading
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a ConfigLoader class that fetches multiple secrets from Azure Key Vault and returns them as a Map. Handle missing secrets gracefully.
prompt_templates.scenario: Loading application configuration from Key Vault

output_examples.input: Store a new API key in Key Vault
output_examples.output: Secret created with name 'api-key' and ID 'https://vault-name.vault.azure.net/secrets/api-key'. The secret is enabled and ready for use.

output_examples.input: List all secrets in the vault
output_examples.output: Found 5 secrets: db-connection-string (enabled, created 2024-01-15), api-key (enabled, expires 2025-01-01), jwt-secret (enabled), smtp-password (disabled), cert-password (enabled)
Previous version of 'db-password' disabled. New version created with ID version 'a1b2c3d4'. Secret rotation completed successfully.

best_practices: Enable soft delete on Key Vault to protect against accidental deletion and allow recovery
Use tags to organize secrets by environment, service, and owner for easier management
Set expiration dates on secrets containing credentials to enforce regular rotation
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secret values in application code instead of retrieving from Key Vault
Logging or printing secret values which may expose them in application logs
Using the same Key Vault for all environments instead of separate vaults per environment

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Key Vault from my Java application?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI credentials, and environment variables. This is the recommended approach for production deployments.

faq.question: Can I update the value of an existing secret?
faq.answer: No, secret values are immutable. To change a secret value, call setSecret() with the same name which creates a new version. You can update properties like enabled status and tags on existing versions.

faq.question: What happens when I delete a secret?
faq.answer: If soft delete is enabled, the secret enters a deleted state and can be recovered for a retention period (7-90 days). After the retention period or if purge protection is disabled, you can permanently purge it.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets that contain JSON or structured data?
faq.answer: Set the contentType property to 'application/json' when creating the secret. This helps identify the format. You can store any string value including JSON, XML, or connection strings.

faq.question: What is the difference between sync and async clients?
faq.answer: The synchronous client blocks until operations complete, suitable for simple applications. The async client uses reactive programming with Project Reactor, better for high-throughput scenarios and non-blocking applications.

faq.question: How do I access a specific version of a secret?
faq.answer: Use getSecret(name, version) with the specific version ID. You can list available versions using listPropertiesOfSecretVersions() to find the version identifier you need.
